title: Uppercase and lowercase code
text: Uppercase and lowercase code You can uppercase and lowercase code in the Uniface IDE using the IDE code editor popup menu. You need to right-click and click Uppercase on the popup menu to uppercase the current selection. Similarly, you need to right-click inside a selection and click Lowercase to lowercase a selected text. You can always undo uppercase and lowercase actions with Ctrl+Z . Additionally, you can use the default shortcut keys defined for Uppercase and Lowercase feature in the [userkeys] section of the usys.ini file. The default shortcut keys defined in the usys.ini file are Ctrl+U to uppercase a selection and Ctrl+Shift+U to lowercase a selected code as defined in the following block. Explain this code [USERKEYS] code.uppercase=Ctrl+U code.lowercase=Ctrl+Shift+U Copy Limitation The Uppercase and Lowercase functions do not work if a selection spans over the declarations section or other sections denoted by their names. These functions only works when a selected code is inside the area defined in the code editor. If you cannot change the case of a selection, the editor will display a message popup.
